title: Burl Ives
content:

{{Quote|Ives's voice … had the sheen and finesse of opera without its latter-day Puccinian vulgarities and without the pretensions of operatic ritual. It was genteel in expressive impact without being genteel in social conformity. And it moved people.|John Rockwell|<ref name="Times">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2006/12/24/books/review/mr-rotten-mr-vicious-and-mr-wagner.html|text=Mr. totten, Mr. Vicious and Mr. Wagner|author=Phillip Lopate|date=2006-12-24|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|The New York Times]]|archivedate=20230511144626}}</ref>}}
'''Burl Ives''' ([[June 14]], [[1900s|1909]] – [[April 14]], [[1995]]) was an [[Wikipedia:Academy Award|Academy Award]]–winning actor, author, and renowned folk singer. Ives established a strong presence for himself on the screen, and was directed to an Academy Award by [[Wikipedia:William Wyler|William Wyler]] for his work in [[Wikipedia:The Big Country|''The Big Country'']].

Ives was also known for his voice work. In [[1984]] he narrated [[John Korty]]'s ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]''. He passed on in [[1995]] due to [[Wikipedia:Oral cancer|oral cancer]].

==Biography==
===Birth===
'''Burl Icle Ivanhoe Ives'''<ref name="amug" /> was born in [[Wikipedia:Hunt City Township, Jasper County, Illinois|Hunt City Township]] in [[Wikipedia:Jasper County, Illinois|Jasper County]], [[Wikipedia:Illinois|Illinois]], on [[June 14]], [[1900s|1909]];<ref name="ss" /><ref name="den">{{WebCite|url=https://thekeep.eiu.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1010&context=den_1995_apr|text=Eastern mourns Burl Ives' death|author=Cole, Betsy|date=1995-04-17|format=[[Wikipedia:PDF|PDF]]|work=[[Wikipedia:The Daily Eastern News|The Daily Eastern News]]|pages=[https://thekeep.eiu.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1010&context=den_1995_apr#page=2 p. 2]|archivedate=20200409183758}}</ref> the youngest of six children.<ref name="Obit">{{WebCite|url=http://www.nytimes.com/1995/04/15/obituaries/burl-ives-the-folk-singer-whose-imposing-acting-won-an-oscar-dies-at-85.html|text=Burl Ives, the Folk Singer Whose Imposing Acting Won an Oscar, Dies at 85|author=Severo, Richard|date=1995-04-15|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|The New York Times]]|archivedate=20090909131556}}</ref> His father was a tenant farmer.<ref name="Parish">[[Wikipedia:James Robert Parish|Parish, James Robert]]; and Michael R. Pitts, eds. "Burl Ives". ''Hollywood Songsters: Singers Who Act and Actors Who Sing: A Biographical Dictionary''. Vol. 2. [[Wikipedia:New York City|New York]]: [[Wikipedia:Routledge|Routledge]], [[2003]]. 403—406.</ref>

===''Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure''===
{{Quote|Our story begins in a time long, long ago, deep in an enchanted forest, on the distant moon of Endor.|Burl Ives as the Narrator|audio=BurlDamnYou.flac|<ref name="CC" />}}
In [[1984]], Ives came out of retirement to provide narration for [[John Korty]]'s ''[[Star Wars]]'' telemovie, ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]''. Ives' narration in the film's opening scenes explained the actions of a group of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] who spoke only [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]]; as the narration continued throughout the course of the film, it provided context to a plot in which a [[Mace Towani|brother]] and [[Cindel Towani|sister]] had to save their [[Towani family|parents]] from a [[Gorax/Legends|giant monster]].<ref name="CC" />

===Death===
[[File:EruditeIves.jpg|thumb|right|120px|An elderly Ives]]
{{Quote|Burl Ives, whose sweet, strong, mournful way with folk ballads made him an international singing star in the 1940's and whose earthy acting won him an Academy Award in the 1950's, died yesterday at his home in Anacortes, Wash. He was 85.|''The New York Times'' obituary, by Richard Severo|<ref name="Obit" />}}
Ives died on [[April 14]], [[1995]] in [[Wikipedia:Anacortes, Washington|Anacortes]], Washington due to [[Wikipedia:Oral cancer|oral cancer]].<ref name="amug" /><ref name="NYPL">{{WebCite|url=http://archives.nypl.org/uploads/collection/pdf_finding_aid/theives.pdf|text=Guide to the Burl Ives Papers, 1913-1975|author=Ives, Burl|format=[[Wikipedia:PDF|PDF]]|work=[http://archives.nypl.org New York Public Library Archives & Manuscripts]|archivedate=20191018022046}}</ref>
